key_insight: Bayesian inference fused with photoluminescence imaging and drift-diffusion modeling can localize where perovskite solar cells degrade—at the bulk or specific interfaces—while tracking how those regions evolve over time.
engineering_relevance: This gives device engineers a practical diagnostic tool for identifying dominant failure modes and validating passivation strategies to improve perovskite solar-cell lifetime.
method: Photoluminescence imaging of fully fabricated perovskite devices
Drift-diffusion simulations coupled to Bayesian inference to infer spatial parameter maps
Temporal tracking of inferred recombination parameters during aging at 70 °C under full-spectrum sunlight
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the inferred maps reveal pronounced spatially non-uniform degradation and distinguish hole/electron transport layer interface degradation from bulk effects, with amino-silane passivation suppressing the worst degradation.
watch_item: The approach relies on model assumptions linking optical signals to drift-diffusion parameters, so inferred maps should be interpreted carefully and validated against independent degradation measurements.
paper_title: Disentangling the origin of degradation in perovskite solar cells via optical imaging and Bayesian inference
